nodes.x: Unto thee lift I up mine eyes, O thou that dwellest in the heavens.
Unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my help.

¶ Then said he unto me, Son of man, lift up thine eyes now the way toward the north. So I lifted up mine eyes the way toward the north, and behold northward at the gate of the altar this image of jealousy in the entry.
But behold, verily, verily, I say unto you that mine eyes are upon you. I am in your midst and ye cannot see me;
And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes wait upon the Lord our God, until that he have mercy upon…
How long shall thy hand be stayed, and thine eye, yea thy pure eye, behold from the eternal heavens the wrongs of thy people and of thy servants, and thine ear be penetrated with their cries?
The Lord is in his holy temple, the Lord’s throne is in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.
The eyes of the Lord are in every place, beholding the evil and the good.
¶ And if a man smite the eye of his servant, or the eye of his maid, that it perish; he shall let him go free for his eye’s sake.
Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me: he that walketh in a perfect way, he shall serve me.
Have mercy upon us, O Lord, have mercy upon us: for we are exceedingly filled with contempt.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.

Verily, verily I say unto you, blessed are such, for they shall obtain; for I, the Lord, show mercy unto all the meek, and upon all whomsoever I will, that I may be justified when I shall bring them unto judgment.
Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scorning of those that are at ease, and with the contempt of the proud.
To usher its broad radiance on a train Of humble, cheerful worshipers. Nature Seem’d conscious of the mournful knell That broke upon the sadden’d heart of man !
Be not ashamed, neither confounded; but be admonished in all your high-mindedness and pride, for it bringeth a snare upon your souls.
The proud have forged a lie against me: but I will keep thy precepts with my whole heart.

I cannot express my feelings; I can imagine, but cannot give vent to my imaginations, when I realize the situation of the Saints in the valleys of these mountains. I expect, if I should give way to my feelings mingled with the weakness pertaining to mankind, that you would call me more foolish than a Methodist, or even more foolish than a right down shouting Ranter. I think that I know how to…
